stories.headline: Tamil Nadu Votes Today in High-Stakes Four-Cornered Battle
stories.summary: Over 5.73 crore voters in Tamil Nadu are casting ballots today across 234 constituencies, choosing between CM M.K. Stalin's ruling DMK alliance, the AIADMK-led opposition, and two new forces — actor Vijay's TVK and Seeman's NTK. Results will be declared on May 4. The election is seen as a test of whether the DMK can overcome anti-incumbency and whether a movie star can break into Tamil Nadu's fiercely competitive politics.
stories.bigWordDef: Being the current person holding a job or office — 'anti-incumbency' means voters are tired of the same party being in power.
stories.thinkQuestion: Should famous actors or celebrities be allowed to enter politics directly? What qualities should a good political leader have?
stories.videoQuery: Tamil Nadu election 2026 explained DMK AIADMK TVK
stories.sourceQuote: The total electoral strength stands at 5.73 crore, comprising 2.93 women, 2.83 crore men, and 7,728 third-gender persons

stories.headline: Jordan Becomes 63rd Country to Sign NASA's Artemis Accords for Space Cooperation
stories.summary: Today at NASA Headquarters in Washington, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan signed the Artemis Accords, a set of principles for the peaceful and responsible exploration of the Moon, Mars, and beyond. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman hosted Ambassador Dina Kawar for the ceremony. Jordan follows Latvia, which signed just three days ago on April 20, as nations continue joining the growing international space cooperation framework.
stories.bigWordDef: An official agreement between countries — the Artemis Accords are like a 'rules of space' treaty that countries agree to follow.
stories.thinkQuestion: Why is it important for countries to agree on rules before exploring space — could space ever become a place for conflict, like territory on Earth?
stories.videoQuery: Artemis Accords explained NASA moon exploration
stories.sourceQuote: The Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 9:30 a.m. EDT Thursday, April 23, at NASA Headquarters in Washington

stories.headline: 15-Year-Old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Smashes IPL Record, Becomes Youngest to Score 500 Runs
stories.summary: Rajasthan Royals' teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player ever to score 500 IPL runs during last night's match against Lucknow Super Giants, overtaking Prithvi Shaw's record. He hit the milestone at just 15 years and 27 days. Rajasthan Royals won the match by 40 runs, with fast bowler Jofra Archer and all-rounder Ravindra Jadeja starring with the ball.
stories.bigWordDef: A young person with extraordinarily exceptional talent or skill — someone who achieves things far beyond what is normal for their age.
stories.thinkQuestion: Vaibhav started serious cricket training at age 4. Is it fair to put so much pressure on young children to become elite sports professionals, or is it just following passion?
stories.sourceQuote: At 15 years and 27 days, Vaibhav breached the 500-run mark, surpassing Prithvi Shaw for the record

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz Stays Nearly Shut as Iran Seizes Ships After Trump Extends Ceasefire
stories.summary: Ship traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz remained almost completely halted on Wednesday even after US President Trump extended his ceasefire with Iran, with Iran's parliament speaker declaring that reopening the strait is 'impossible' as long as the US naval blockade of Iranian ports continues. Only about eight ships crossed the strait, compared to a normal flow of hundreds daily. The ongoing disruption has triggered what experts call the largest oil supply shock in history.
stories.bigWordDef: When a country uses its military to prevent ships from entering or leaving another country's ports — like a wall at sea.
stories.thinkQuestion: Energy crises caused by wars far away can raise fuel and food prices everywhere in the world. How should countries think about reducing their dependence on oil from conflict zones?
stories.videoQuery: Strait of Hormuz blockade oil prices impact India
stories.sourceQuote: Iran's attacks on oil tankers before the ceasefire led to a plunge in traffic through the strait, triggering what's been described as the largest oil supply disruption in history

stories.headline: Iran Attacks Ships in Hormuz as Trump Extends Ceasefire — But Keeps Naval Blockade
stories.summary: President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire on April 21, just hours before it was set to expire, giving Tehran more time to submit a 'unified proposal' for ending the war. However, the US naval blockade of Iranian ports continues, and Iran responded by firing on three commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran says the blockade violates the ceasefire, creating a dangerous standoff.
stories.bigWordDef: When a military force seals off a port or area so that ships and supplies cannot get in or out — it's like putting a giant lock on a country's doorway to the sea.
stories.thinkQuestion: If both sides say the other is violating the ceasefire, how can negotiations ever get started? What role can a third country like Pakistan play?
stories.videoQuery: Strait of Hormuz importance global oil shipping explained
stories.sourceQuote: Iran has insisted that the blockade represents a violation of the ceasefire, and has said it will not negotiate under the shadow of threats

videos.title: System of Elections in India Explained | Social Studies Class 6 | CBSE | NCERT | AASOKA
videos.description: A clear explainer on how India's massive democratic elections function — EVMs, voter rolls, constituencies, and how governments are formed. Perfect for understanding today's Tamil Nadu vote.

videos.title: Artemis II Explained | 2026 Mission to the Moon
videos.description: NASA's Artemis II crew recently became the first humans to fly around the Moon in 50 years. This video explains the whole Artemis programme and what comes next — including a crewed Moon landing.
videos.searchQuery: NASA Artemis II moon mission 2026 explained

videos.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz Is So Important
videos.description: A geography and economics explainer on why this narrow strip of water between Iran and Oman controls a fifth of the world's oil — and what happens when it gets blocked.
videos.searchQuery: Strait of Hormuz explained why important oil
